summary: Implementation correctly addresses all acceptance criteria. The profile handler is made async, guest emails are fetched server-side with proper error handling, ownerPrivateKey display is removed, share section renders conditionally on ownerAddress, and JS functions follow existing click-to-copy pattern. All 13 tests pass. Minor findings below.

findings.description: createInvite() and revokeInvite() share ~90% identical code (innerHTML assignment, click-to-copy handler, resultEl.style.display, error alert). Consider extracting a shared helper like showInviteUrl(d.url) to reduce duplication.
findings.reason: Follows the same pattern as generateMagicLink() in the same file which is also self-contained. Extracting a helper inside an inline <script> in a template literal adds complexity without significant benefit. The duplication is contained and understandable.

findings.description: createInvite() and revokeInvite() do not handle the case where the endpoint returns 403 (ownerAddress not set) differently from other errors. The user sees a generic 'Ошибка' alert for both 403 and network errors.
findings.reason: The share section is only rendered when ownerAddress is set, so 403 from these endpoints should not normally occur in practice. Generic error message is acceptable for edge cases.
